verses:
rāi⋅kānu jamunāra mājhe
phiraye taranī, jalera ghūraṇī,
dūra gela kula lāje

kumbhīra makara, mīna uṭhata,
saghane vadana tuli
hariṣe ṣamunā, uthale dviguṇa,
rāi kānu rūpe bhuli

kahaye lalitā, haye sacakitā,
śuna lo mukharā buḍī
tomāri kathāya, caḍi bhāṅgā nāya,
parāṇa sahita mari

mukharā kahaye, jā māge kāṇḍārī,
tāhāi karaha dāna
e bhāṅgā taraṇī, pāra hare ekhani,
kena vā jāibe prāṇa

e saba vacana, śuniyā kāṇḍārī,
kahaye lalitā pāśe
tomāra sakhīra, paraśa māgiye,
vamśī śuniyā hāse
en_translation:
Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa are in the middle of the Yamunā. The boat turns while the water swirls, and they have no shame regarding family honor.

Crocodiles, alligators, and fish rise up, frequently lifting their faces to see. The Yamunā overflows twofold in joy, enchanted by the beauty of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa.

Lalitā says, becoming alarmed: "Listen, O talkative old woman! Because of your words, we have boarded this broken boat, and I fear we will lose our lives."

The old woman replies: "Whatever the boatman asks for, just give it to him. This broken boat will cross now; why should your lives be lost?"

Hearing these words, the boatman says to Lalitā: "I am only asking for the touch of your friend." He laughs upon hearing the sound of His own flute.
